title: campanula glomerata subsp. elliptica
intro: Campanula glomerata subsp. elliptica, commonly known as the clustered bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing swelling and fighting off infections, and it has been used to treat conditions like wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Campanula glomerata subsp. elliptica include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
